key_facts.Network: BNB Chain and multiple supported chains

intent.a: PancakeSwap started on BNB Chain and is still most closely associated with BNB Smart Chain trading, but it is no longer a single-chain DEX. PancakeSwap documentation describes the product as available across multiple chains, including BNB Chain, Ethereum, Solana, Base, Arbitrum, Aptos, ZKsync, Linea, Monad, and opBNB. The exact route a user sees depends on the token pair, wallet network, and whether the app is using same-chain swap, cross-chain swap, or a specific product such as Infinity. BNB Chain remains the key chain for many CAKE pairs and legacy PancakeSwap users.

intent.q: PancakeSwap BNB Chain fees and swap costs
intent.a: A PancakeSwap trade on BNB Chain includes the network gas fee paid in BNB plus the DEX pool or route fee embedded in the swap quote. Gas on BNB Chain is usually small for normal swaps, but the effective cost also includes price impact, slippage, and any routing through multiple pools. PancakeSwap supports several trading products, so fees can differ by pool version, stable-swap route, Infinity pool, or cross-chain swap. Before confirming, users should check minimum received, route details, gas token balance, and whether the wallet is on BNB Chain or another supported chain.

intent.q: PancakeSwap supported chains for CAKE and swaps
intent.a: PancakeSwap supports swaps beyond BNB Chain, including Ethereum, Solana, Base, Arbitrum, Aptos, ZKsync, Linea, Monad, and opBNB according to its current product documentation. CAKE itself can appear on multiple networks through official deployments or bridging arrangements, so the token contract address matters. A CAKE balance on BNB Chain is not automatically the same as a CAKE balance on Ethereum or Aptos. Users should switch the wallet to the intended network, confirm the token address from PancakeSwap or a reputable explorer, and avoid lookalike CAKE tokens promoted in search ads.

PancakeSwap V3 introduced concentrated liquidity and multiple fee tiers, while PancakeSwap Infinity is the newer architecture designed for more flexible pool types and routing. For everyday traders, the practical question is execution quality: which route gives the best output after pool fees, price impact, and gas. For liquidity providers, the difference is more important because pool design, position management, and incentives can change returns and risk. Users do not need to manually pick every contract in most app flows, but they should understand that PancakeSwap is a suite of products, not only one BNB Chain router.

intent.q: PancakeSwap CAKE staking and chain choice
intent.a: CAKE staking and PancakeSwap chain selection are related but not identical. Swapping can happen on several supported chains, while CAKE staking or reward products may be available only in specific app sections and networks. A user who wants to stake CAKE should check the official PancakeSwap Earn documentation and confirm the wallet network before approving tokens. Yield rates are not fixed forever; they can depend on emissions, fee sharing, pool rules, and governance changes. Treat CAKE staking as a product-specific DeFi action, not simply as a feature available equally on every PancakeSwap-supported chain.

Ethereum blue chips and cross-chain liquidity
Higher gas and duplicate token symbols

faq.q: Is PancakeSwap only on BNB Chain?
faq.a: No. PancakeSwap began on BNB Chain, but the current app supports several chains. BNB Chain remains central to PancakeSwap's identity and CAKE liquidity, while other networks expand access to assets and users. Always confirm the selected chain in the wallet before swapping.

faq.q: What gas token do I need for PancakeSwap?
faq.a: The gas token depends on the selected chain. On BNB Chain you need BNB, on Ethereum you need ETH, on Base you need ETH on Base, and on Solana you need SOL. CAKE is not the universal gas token for PancakeSwap trades.

faq.q: Can I use PancakeSwap with MetaMask?
faq.a: Yes. MetaMask works with EVM networks supported by PancakeSwap, including BNB Chain, Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, ZKsync, Linea, and opBNB. For non-EVM networks such as Solana or Aptos, users need a compatible wallet for that ecosystem.

faq.q: Why is my CAKE on the wrong chain?
faq.a: CAKE can exist on more than one network. If your wallet is connected to a chain where your CAKE is not held, the balance may not appear or may refer to a different token contract. Switch to the correct chain and verify the contract address from official sources.

PancakeSwap swaps are non-custodial when executed from a self-custody wallet. The app and smart contracts route the trade, but the user signs transactions from their own wallet. Token approvals still matter because they grant contracts permission to spend selected ERC-20 or BEP-20 tokens.

faq.q: Where can I check PancakeSwap audits?
faq.a: PancakeSwap maintains an official audits page listing reports for products such as Infinity, Exchange V3, MasterChef, StableSwap, and other components. Audits reduce unknowns but do not remove all smart-contract, governance, liquidity, or user-signing risk.
